title: Lithocarpus rotundatus
taxonomy: Lithocarpus rotundatus is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family Fagaceae. It is native to southern China. The classification of Lithocarpus rotundatus is as a species of the genus Lithocarpus within the family Fagaceae. The genus Lithocarpus is a group of evergreen trees and shrubs closely related to the beech genus Fagus. As a member of the Fagaceae family, Lithocarpus rotundatus is also part of the broader order Fagales.
uses: Lithocarpus rotundatus is native to Taiwan and has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. The plant's leaves and bark have been used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. In traditional Taiwanese medicine, Lithocarpus rotundatus is used to lower blood pressure and improve cardiovascular health. The plant's extracts have also been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal potential of Lithocarpus rotundatus.
benefits: Lithocarpus rotundatus, a type of evergreen tree native to Asia, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. Its leaves and seeds are believed to possess antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce fever and alleviate respiratory issues. The plant also contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are known to have antioxidant and anti-cancer effects. properties: Lithocarpus rotundatus, a plant species native to China and Southeast Asia, has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its reported therapeutic properties. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its potential health benefits. Studies have shown that extracts from Lithocarpus rotundatus exhibit inhibitory effects on certain bacterial strains and may have applications in the development of novel antimicrobial agents. parts: Lithocarpus rotundatus, also known as the Himalayan copper beech, has been used in traditional medicine. The leaves, bark, and fruits of the plant are used to treat various health issues. The leaves are said to have anti-inflammatory properties and are used to treat fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal problems. The bark is used to treat skin conditions and wounds. preparations: Lithocarpus rotundatus is a plant species native to Southeast Asia, and its herbal preparations are used in traditional medicine. side_effects: Lithocarpus rotundatus, commonly known as the Burmese tanak, is a plant native to Asia and is used in traditional medicine. intro: Lithocarpus rotundatus, also known as the round-leaf Lithocarpus, is a species of evergreen tree in the beech family (Fagaceae). The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it beneficial in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease, diabetes, and certain types of cancer. The bioactive constituents of Lithocarpus rotundatus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Lithocarpus rotundatus is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
